name: Searching
description: Questions on linear search, binary search, and search patterns.

questions.question: Binary search on a sorted list of n elements has time complexity:
questions.explanation: Binary search halves the search space each step, so it runs in O(log n) comparisons.

questions.question: For binary search, the input must be:
questions.explanation: Binary search relies on the ordering of elements to eliminate half the space each step.

questions.question: x in list for a Python list does:
questions.explanation: Membership in a list is O(n) linear search; there is no assumption of ordering.

questions.question: bisect_left([1,2,2,3], 2) returns:
questions.explanation: bisect_left returns the leftmost index where 2 can be inserted to keep order; that index is 1.

questions.question: Which data structure gives O(1) average membership test?
questions.explanation: Sets use hashing for O(1) average membership; lists and tuples are O(n).
